mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already near zero, the market interprets policy as supportive and discount rates stay suppressed, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as stimulus rather than panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down and CPIAUCSL inflation is not rising, or if the conditional hit rate stays below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent floor signals labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should appear only after the unemployment rate breaks upward from its prior 6-month range.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points above its 6-month minimum
failure_condition: Reject if upward breaks in UNRATE fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average 20-day return after triggers is non-negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which improves near-term corporate cash-flow expectations and supports equity prices over the next 5 trading days. The signal is most useful when sales growth is broad rather than a one-month noise spike.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases year-over-year for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive year-over-year gains in RSAFS are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if rank correlation between RSAFS change and forward returns is below 0.02 in any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable employment reflects an expansion in real output without immediate labor stress, which reduces recession fears and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. If production rises while payrolls contract, the mechanism fails because the output gain is not translating into broad demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if the combined condition produces negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST from a prior peak is an early housing-led warning of slowing demand and tighter credit transmission, which should pressure equity valuations over the next quarter. The effect should be negative only after housing starts turn down from elevated levels, not during already depressed housing conditions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 3-month high
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start drawdowns fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the conditional average return after triggers is positive over any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low FEDFUNDS and easing CPIAUCSL inflation signals real policy accommodation, which should lift equity multiples and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is weakest when inflation is accelerating, because then low nominal rates do not imply true easing.
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate and disinflation periods do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than low-rate periods alone, or if the interaction term has no positive incremental effect in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market weakening shock that directly reduces expected earnings and increases risk premia, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal should activate only when both employment measures move in the same adverse direction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the combined signal underperforms either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with falling RSAFS identifies broad demand exhaustion, which should depress equity prices over the next 60 trading days through lower revenue and earnings expectations. The mechanism fails when GDP slows but retail sales remain resilient, because then the slowdown is not broad-based.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below 1% annualized and RSAFS is declining year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDP plus declining retail sales does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average forward return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean in any 5-year rolling window.
